Home›Services›Defense Advisory› ITAR
Export Control

ITAR Cybersecurity
Compliance

International Traffic in Arms Regulations impose strict requirements on how defense-related technical data is stored, transmitted, and accessed. Our practice ensures your cybersecurity infrastructure meets ITAR requirements while supporting operational efficiency.

Securing Export-Controlled Data

ITAR violations carry severe civil and criminal penalties — including fines of up to $1 million per violation and potential prison sentences. For defense and aerospace organizations, ensuring that technical data related to defense articles is properly protected is a fundamental business requirement.

Our team implements cybersecurity controls specifically designed for ITAR compliance: access restrictions ensuring only authorized U.S. persons access controlled data, encrypted communications for technical data sharing, comprehensive audit logging, and monitoring systems that detect and alert on policy violations.

Capabilities

  • ITAR data classification and marking procedures
  • U.S. Person access control implementation and verification
  • Encrypted storage and transmission for controlled technical data
  • Cloud infrastructure compliance assessment for ITAR workloads
  • Third-party and subcontractor data sharing controls
  • Audit logging and access monitoring for ITAR systems
  • ITAR compliance training for technical staff
  • Incident response procedures for potential ITAR violations
  • Integration with CMMC and NIST 800-171 compliance programs

Who We Serve

Defense and aerospace manufacturers handling ITAR-controlled technical data. Engineering firms with export-controlled design files. IT departments managing infrastructure for ITAR-restricted programs. Companies that have received a Directorate of Defense Trade Controls inquiry.

ITAR Controls

  • Access Restriction
  • Data Encryption
  • Audit Monitoring
  • Cloud Compliance
  • Vendor Controls
  • Training Programs
